column1: # wsel17-cleanup-manifest.tsv -- WSEL-17 Step 5/6 disposition inventory (width strand, THE GRAND CLEANUP).
# WHAT THIS IS. One row per width driver, architecture module, and results-JSON group, each run
# through the spec's four MECHANICAL eligibility checks. DELETE-ELIGIBLE requires ALL FOUR to hold;
# everything else is KEEP or SHIM. This task (Steps 1-6) DELETES NOTHING -- Step 7 (attended,
# root + user sign-off) is the only step authorised to remove a file, and only after reading this
# THE FOUR CHECKS (columns 6-9), each run as a command, output recorded in the cell:
#   c2_cited       -- cited by a LIVE plan file. "Live plan file" = the citation gate's OWN walk
#                     every .md/.tsv under docs/plans/capacity_programme/, EXCLUDING `archive/` and any
#                     `reviews_*/` subdirectory (both are explicitly frozen-record dirs per that function).
#                     Checked via: python3 walk matching that exact predicate, then `grep -l -F <name>`
#                     over the resulting file list (25 files at execution time, 2026-07-22).
#   c3_imported    -- a GENUINE import statement (`grep -rnE '^\s*(import|from)\s+<module>\b'`), not a
#                     bare filename substring match -- a filename appearing in a docstring/comment
#                     ("this replaces X.py") is NOT an import and is called out as such below where it
#                     was the only hit a naive substring grep would have returned.
#   c4_superseded  -- a NAMED on-disk replacement exists (path written in the row); "looks old" alone
# METHODOLOGY NOTE ON GRANULARITY. Results JSONs are grouped ONE ROW PER PRODUCER/RESULTS-DIRECTORY
# (not one row per of the ~719 individual per-seed/per-cell leaf files under
# automl_package/examples/capacity_ladder_results/{W*,WSEL*}/) wherever every file in that directory
# shares one producer script, one citation pattern, and one disposition -- e.g. WSEL4/ alone holds 145
# leaf JSONs, all current cells of the same ANSWERED task, all KEEP for the same reason. Individual rows
# are given ONLY where a specific file's disposition genuinely differs from its directory-mates (W_MRL's
# two files; the WSEL11 voided-vs-rerun split, which width.md itself already treats as two artifacts).
# This keeps the manifest reviewable; it trades off NOT naming each of the 719 leaves individually. If
# finer granularity is wanted, re-run with each directory's `find <dir> -name '*.json'` expanded.
# HEADSTONE FINDING (read before trusting any DELETE-ELIGIBLE row -- there are none in this manifest).
# directory, so the citation gate's own walk treats it as a live plan file. That document's entire
# purpose is enumerating near-zero-caller candidates BY NAME -- so under the mechanical rule AS
# LITERALLY SPECIFIED, its own act of discussing a file as "maybe dead" is itself a citation that BLOCKS
# check c2 for every file it discusses. This makes check c2 nearly untriggerable for exactly the
# marginal files Step 5 is supposed to catch. Two rows below (independent_width_experiment.py/W_INDEP,
# and w_mrl_summary_run1_partial.json) hit this exactly: checks c1/c3/c4 all independently support
# DELETE-ELIGIBLE, but c2 is blocked ONLY by zero-caller-inventory.md's own self-referential mention.
# Recorded as KEEP (mechanical rule applied literally, all four required) with the near-miss called out
# for the root's judgement at Step 7 -- this task does not override a mechanical check with its own
# discretion (Step 5's own instruction: "ELIGIBILITY IS MECHANICAL, NOT A JUDGEMENT").
